title: SOC 2 CC7 — Operational Anomaly Detection on Critical Batch and API SLOs

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → 'A1.2'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) or MLTK
spl:
index=app sourcetype="api:gateway" service="payments_api" earliest=-60d
| timechart span=1h perc95(latency_ms) as p95_lat, sum(eval(if(status>=500,1,0))) as err, count as vol
| eventstats median(vol) as med_vol, stdev(vol) as sd_vol
| eval z=if(sd_vol>0, (vol-med_vol)/sd_vol, 0)
| where abs(z)>4
| table _time, vol, med_vol, z, p95_lat, err
value: CC7 includes monitoring for anomalies. Statistical deviation on transaction volumes, error rates, or job durations for financially relevant pipelines shows detective monitoring beyond static thresholds—supporting availability and processing-integrity narratives.
implementation: (1) Scope `service` to in-scope subsystems; (2) tune z-score window; (3) suppress known marketing events via lookup; (4) feed notable to on-call; (5) document model refresh cadence for auditors.
visualization: Line chart (vol with bands), Table (anomaly hours), Single value (count last 7d).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(Performance.cpu_load_percent) as agg_value from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host span=1h | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
